suggested_fixes: Optionally call cublasSetPointerMode(handle, CUBLAS_POINTER_MODE_HOST) before SGEMM calls if you want to make host scalar pointer usage explicit.
Keep the current cuBLAS parameter patterns: SGEMM1 N/N with A=X and B=W, SGEMM2 N/T with C=U shaped {d,16}, and SGEMM3 N/N with beta=1 accumulation into Y.
warnings: Inputs are made contiguous via W.contiguous(), X.contiguous(), A.contiguous(), and B.contiguous(); this is safe and handles non-contiguous inputs, but may allocate/copy at runtime.

The code does not explicitly set cuBLAS pointer mode to host before passing host alpha/beta pointers. This is typically safe with PyTorch-managed cuBLAS handles, but explicitly setting CUBLAS_POINTER_MODE_HOST would make the assumption clearer.
